pi_items.source: Task definition requires two independent product systems (coal and natural gas electricity). Technically feasible with USLCI database which contains both coal and natural gas electricity generation processes.

USLCI database confirmed to contain electricity processes for both coal-fired power plants and natural gas combined cycle (NGCC) plants. Verified via NREL/FLCAC documentation and GitHub repository (https://github.com/FLCAC-admin/uslci-content).

Both Global Warming Potential (GWP) and Acidification Potential (AP) are standard impact categories in TRACI v2.1 methodology, which is the primary LCIA method being used. Verified via EPA TRACI documentation and TRACI v2.1 for FEDEFLv1.2 dataset (Figshare article 25782786).
Task explicitly requires exporting scenario comparison to this CSV file. Path is consistent across task.json, README.md, and setup/export scripts.
Task setup script creates this directory at /home/ga/LCA_Results and verifies file output to this location. Consistent across all task scripts.

pi_items.verified_value: GitHub FLCAC-admin/uslci-content (FY24 Q4 or FY25 Q1 JSON-LD release)
pi_items.source: Installation script downloads USLCI from: https://github.com/FLCAC-admin/uslci-content/raw/dev/downloads/uslci_fy24_q4_01_olca2_4_0_elci_lib_json_ld.zip with fallback to FY25 Q1. This is the real, publicly available U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database published by NREL.

pi_items.verified_value: Figshare - TRACIv2.1 for FEDEFLv1.2 (article 25782786) via Ag Data Commons
pi_items.source: Installation script downloads LCIA methods from Figshare article 25782786, which provides TRACI v2.1 impact characterization factors compatible with openLCA and USLCI database flows. Verified at: https://agdatacommons.nal.usda.gov/articles/dataset/TRACIv2_1_for_FEDEFLv1_2_No_Flows_/25782786

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real, publicly available LCA databases: the USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database) version FY24 Q4 or FY25 Q1 from NREL/FLCAC and TRACI v2.1 LCIA methods from Figshare/USDA. The USLCI database contains coal-fired power plant and natural gas combined cycle (NGCC) electricity generation processes with real life cycle inventory data. Both required impact categories—Global Warming Potential (GWP) and Acidification Potential (AP)—are available in the TRACI v2.1 methodology. The task requires creating two independent product systems and comparing their environmental impacts with a percentage reduction calculation, outputting results to a CSV file.
